implicit_deps: When Step._should_include(parent) returns (False, None), Step.include(parent) does not call Step.create(parent), does not append the step instance to parent.steps, and only records the step in parent._finalize_steps if not already present.
Step instances are created earlier during Blueprint.claim_steps via step = S(parent, **kwargs), so Step.__init__ is still called even if include_if returns False later.
The exclusion decision happens during Blueprint.apply -> step.include(parent), after instantiation but before parent.steps population.

raw_output: {"ground_truth":{"direct_deps":["celery/bootsteps.py:Step._should_include","celery/bootsteps.py:Blueprint.apply","celery/bootsteps.py:Blueprint.claim_steps","celery/bootsteps.py:Step.include","celery/bootsteps.py:Step.__init__","celery/bootsteps.py:Step.create"],"indirect_deps":[],"implicit_deps":["When Step._should_include(parent) returns (False, None), Step.include(parent) does not call Step.create(parent), does not append the step instance to parent.steps, and only records the step in parent._finalize_steps if not already present.","Step instances are created earlier during Blueprint.claim_steps via step = S(parent, **kwargs), so Step.__init__ is still called even if include_if returns False later.","The exclusion decision happens during Blueprint.apply -> step.include(parent), after instantiation but before parent.steps population."]}}

indirect_deps: celery/app/task.py:Task signature/request handling and errback execution read request.delivery_info (e.g. routing/exchange/routing_key metadata)
celery/canvas.py:errback/signature application chain may propagate or inspect request context when invoking linked error callbacks
implicit_deps: An empty delivery_info means no routing metadata is available in the fake request context
During errback execution, code expecting request.delivery_info fields such as exchange/routing_key/priority may fail or route incorrectly because the dict is empty
This especially affects errbacks executed from backend-created fake requests, where request-dependent retry/republish semantics cannot be reconstructed

raw_output: {"ground_truth":{"direct_deps":["celery/backends/base.py:_create_fake_task_request sets Context.delivery_info to {} by default"],"indirect_deps":["celery/app/task.py:Task signature/request handling and errback execution read request.delivery_info (e.g. routing/exchange/routing_key metadata)","celery/canvas.py:errback/signature application chain may propagate or inspect request context when invoking linked error callbacks"],"implicit_deps":["An empty delivery_info means no routing metadata is available in the fake request context","During errback execution, code expecting request.delivery_info fields such as exchange/routing_key/priority may fail or route incorrectly because the dict is empty","This especially affects errbacks executed from backend-created fake requests, where request-dependent retry/republish semantics cannot be reconstructed"]}}
